claim: The repository was polished into a presentation-ready state in Session 24 for the user (Naresh Chhillar, B.Sc. Biomedical Sciences, York University) to use in summer/fall wet-lab internship applications. Top-level README.md is now wet-lab-supervisor-readable (~2 pages, leads with simulator + Syn3A + Breuer 2019 framing, ends with three concrete wet-lab-testable hypotheses derived from the simulator's predictions). RESULTS.md is the longer scientific summary (~3-4 pages, professor-audience). figures/ holds plot-ready CSV data + 3 matplotlib generation scripts (mcc_progression, detector_contributions, confusion_matrices) so the user can produce publication-quality PNGs locally without committing image binaries. PROJECT_STATUS.md was reorganised so current state lives at the top above a horizontal rule and the original 22-session log is preserved below for audit-trail continuity. NEXT_SESSION.md was set to 'ship-applications mode' with deferred research directions (multi-organism essentiality, wet-lab FP audit, parameter sensitivity) listed but not active. No simulator code, no notebooks, no script source files, and no measured-fact JSONs were modified in this session — the polish is documentation-only.
source_detail: Reproducible by inspecting the four polish artifacts at HEAD: README.md (~165 lines), RESULTS.md (~135 lines), figures/ (3 CSVs + 3 .py scripts + figures/README.md), PROJECT_STATUS.md (current-state header + 'Original project documentation' divider above the original content), NEXT_SESSION.md (Status: SHIP-APPLICATIONS MODE header). Pre-flight 235 sandbox tests pass; invariant checker reports OK with 47 measured facts and 14 structural facts after this fact lands.

README_md.audience: wet-lab biology supervisor reading an internship application
README_md.leads_with: first-person identification + Syn3A + Breuer 2019 + headline MCC 0.5372
README_md.wet_lab_framing: three concrete experiments the simulator's predictions enable: 3-FP knockout audit, CRISPRi screen of the 96-FN Uncharacterized pool, growth-curve experiment for trxA/hupA/recR late failures

RESULTS_md.audience: professor or research scientist clicked through from README
RESULTS_md.covers: background, three-layer approach, headline metric, MCC progression v10-v15, three documented negative results, comparison to Breuer + Thornburg, limitations, what-next directions
honest_framing_examples: comparable to but does not exceed Breuer 2019 FBA (MCC 0.59)
knowledge-based detectors carry the bulk of the result (composed 0.537 vs trajectory-only 0.13 ceiling)
the simulator cannot predict non-metabolic essentials without the knowledge-based detectors

data_csvs: figures/data/detector_contributions.csv (7 detectors with isolated MCC + role)
figures/data/confusion_matrices.csv (6 full-panel versions + Breuer FBA reference)
figures_directory.no_pre_rendered_pngs: user generates locally; figures/output/ is gitignored
figures_directory.matplotlib_runtime_check: scripts not executed in sandbox (matplotlib not installed); user runs locally

PROJECT_STATUS_reorganized.current_state_includes: headline MCC + confusion + branch + HEAD + tests + invariants + comparison + 3 negative results + 3 what-next options + presentation status

what_was_NOT_changed: scripts/ — no script source files modified (figures/scripts/ are NEW files, not modifications)
cell_sim/tests/ — no test added or removed; 235 still pass
MCC numbers — every figure on the plots cites a fact JSON
user_workflow_post_session: fill in email placeholder in README.md contact section
run figures/scripts/plot_*.py locally to produce PNG/PDF outputs
iterate on README/RESULTS framing per specific application target

caveats: Polish documents reflect the project's state as of commit f1a1cc2 (Session 22 toxicity halt). Any future research session that changes the headline MCC or invalidates a negative-result claim must update README + RESULTS + figures CSVs to stay in sync. The structural fact above will then need a v1.1.0 update or replacement.
The README ends with a contact-email placeholder ('<your-email-here>') that the user must fill in before sending applications. Codex deliberately did not invent an email.

The matplotlib scripts were syntax-checked + compile cleanly but were NOT executed in the Claude Code sandbox because matplotlib is not installed there. The user is expected to run them locally; the data CSVs were verified parseable.

The 'three-FP wet-lab audit' framing in the README cites JCVISYN3A_0034 explicitly but does not name the other two FP locus tags by name; the user can extract them from outputs/predictions_parallel_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1_w4_composed_all455_v15_round2_priors.csv if a specific application requires them.

The invariant checker is run with 47 measured + 14 structural facts after this fact lands. If any future fact deletion drops a dependency this fact relies on (mcc_against_breuer_v15_round2_priors, mcc_tier1_xgboost_naive_stack, toxicity_gate_b_halted_negative_finding, feature_cache_infrastructure) the checker will fail and this fact will need its dependencies list updated.
